visualization.description: Bar chart showing number of prerequisites per course.
visualization.title: Number of prerequisites per course

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  c.id AS course_id,
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  COUNT(p.prerequisite_course_id) AS prereq_count
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.prerequisites AS p
  ON p.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prereq_count DESC, course_code ASC;

issues: X-axis may become crowded with many courses (26 rows reported); consider rotating labels, using a horizontal bar chart, or showing only the top N courses.

The SQL orders results server-side and the Vega-Lite encoding also sorts the nominal x by prereq_count — they are consistent, but maintain just one source of truth for ordering if you later paginate or filter.

result.sql:
SELECT
  c.id AS course_id,
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  COUNT(p.prerequisite_course_id) AS prereq_count
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.prerequisites AS p
  ON p.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prereq_count DESC, course_code ASC;

issues: X-axis may become crowded with many courses (26 rows reported); consider rotating labels, using a horizontal bar chart, or showing only the top N courses.

The SQL orders results server-side and the Vega-Lite encoding also sorts the nominal x by prereq_count — they are consistent, but maintain just one source of truth for ordering if you later paginate or filter.
